modules.name: bubot.Ocf.OcfMessage
documentation: from aiocoap import Message, NON, Code
from bubot.core.Coap.coap import Message, NON, Code

methods.name: bubot.Ocf.OcfMessage.OcfMessage.__init__
documentation:
---
param cn: Information specific to the operation.
param fr: The URI of the message originator.
param kwargs: '?'
param query: '?'
param ri: The identifier that uniquely identifies the message in the originator and
  the recipient.
param to: The URI of the recipient of the message.
---

methods.name: bubot.Ocf.OcfMessage.OcfResponse.is_successful
documentation: True if the code is in the successful subrange of the response code range

modules.name: bubot.core
documentation: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='IoT framework based on OCF specification')
parser.add_argument('--i', dest='di', type=str, help='ID of the device being launched, optional')
parser.add_argument('--p', dest='path', default='./', type=str, help='path to config dir (default: ./)')
# parser.add_argument('--l', default='./', type=str, help='path to log dir (default: ./)')
print(f'Error: class_name {args.class_name} not found. run "bubot -h" for help')

return: true
---
